title: Ying

🇬🇧 ACCA Affiliate (ACCA) 📑

title: Projects
text:
KPMG Shanghai | Audit Associate

Supported audit work related to the preparation of combined carve-out financial statements for client for the periods ending December 2024, 2023, and 2022. The carve-out financial statements were prepared in accordance with accounting policies under IFRS and may be used in connection with a potential primary dual listing in the UK and the Netherlands, with a secondary listing in the US.
My work covered key carve-out areas including the split and testing of inventory provision, PPE, intangible assets, and right-of-use assets, as well as the review of IC sales contracts and trade terms. I also supported substantive procedures over rebates, trade spend, COGS, payroll, other operating costs, and selected revenue workstreams.
Significant procedures included rebate accrual vouching for top customers, retrospective search for unrecorded rebates, TR sub-ledger to general ledger reconciliation, COGS trend and predictive analysis using SAP data, expense trend analysis, payroll substantive analytics, PPE depreciation analytical procedures, and audit support for revenue items not matched under 3-way match procedures.

IFRS Carve-out FS Group Audit PPE / IA / ROU Rebates COGS Payroll Revenue





KPMG Shanghai | Internal Control Testing

Performed SOX controls testing over journal entries and inventory-related controls, with focus on design effectiveness, operating effectiveness, and remediation status at year end. Key risk areas included manual journal entries that were incomplete, inaccurate, or not reviewed at the appropriate management level, as well as inaccurate inventory values or cost of sales caused by insufficient variance review and lack of appropriate corrective action.
Identified control deficiencies included BlackLine journal entry approval controls performed by personnel without sufficient authority or competence, and material consumption variance analysis controls that were not properly designed to meet the underlying control objective. Causes of deficiency included lack of rigorous review and documentation, and insufficient management consideration of appropriate tolerance levels.
I also supported journal entry testing with emphasis on unusual debit or credit entries to cash and borrowing accounts.

SOX 404 COSO JE Testing BlackLine Control Deficiency Inventory Controls Remediation
